questions.prompt: What does PostgREST give you for free?

choices.text: A REST API generated from your Postgres schema: every table becomes an endpoint, every column queryable. SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE with rich filter operators (eq, gt, in, contains, etc.), foreign-key-based embedding for joins in one request, upserts, RPC for calling SQL functions. Authentication via JWT in the Authorization header; authorization enforced by Row Level Security policies in the database. For ~80% of CRUD operations, you don't write API code.

questions.explanation: Auto-generated CRUD REST API from schema; RLS handles authz.

questions.prompt: Need to fetch posts with their authors and comments (with comment authors) in one request. How?

choices.text: Use PostgREST embedded resources via foreign-key traversal: `.select('id, title, author:author_id(id, username), comments(id, body, author:author_id(username))')`. One HTTP request, one SQL query under the hood. Solves N+1 cleanly. Aliases like `author:author_id` rename the embedded field; nested embedding works recursively as long as foreign keys are defined.

questions.explanation: Embedded resources via FK traversal in one `.select()` call.
